preamble: The transformation from a palatalized voiceless alveolar plosive to a voiceless retroflex affricate is a documented phonetic shift, often occurring as a result of palatalization processes.
phoneticEffects: This shift involves affrication (plosive to affricate), a change in place of articulation from alveolar to retroflex, and is often triggered by a palatalizing environment

examples.note: J-Palatalization in Polish can transform an underlying alveolar plosive /t/ into a postalveolar (retroflex) affricate.

sources: Zygis, M., & Pompino-Marschall, B. (1999). The articulation of secondarily palatalized coronals in Polish. In Proceedings of XIVth International Congress of Phonetic Sciences, 1897–1900. San Francisco.
